title: leucas mollissima subsp. acuminata
intro: Leucas mollissima subsp. acuminata, also known as Acuminata or Indian leucas, is a plant species within the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its distinctive leaf shape and fragrance. Traditionally, this plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Leucas mollissima subsp. acuminata include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and providing relief from skin irritations. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are often used in traditional Indian medicine.
